own_review_points: Dialect not registered in CommunityDatabase/CommunityDialectSelector - not discoverable by short name
Many column-type / JDBC-type registrations duplicate what the super Dialect already does (NCLOB, nvarchar, blob/clob, char, float, UUID)
TIMESTAMP/TIMESTAMP_UTC mapping likely wrong - timestamp2 is the real SQL timestamp; LOCAL_DATE_TIME is virtual
NCLOB should map to clob since dialect lacks nclob support
No MINIMUM_VERSION constant / getMinimumSupportedVersion() override; default ctor should pass minimum version
Mutable instance fields should be removed in favor of locals/direct use
Code style: always use blocks for if, final local vars, whitespace
Functions registered with '%' prefix likely invalid Java identifier start - untested registrations
Many registered functions duplicate HQL standard alternatives (extract, upper) - unnecessary
LimitHandler.INSTANCE should be returned (singleton) rather than constructing
Deprecated render()/processSql APIs used instead of current overloads
notes: v8 landed four genuine, substantive findings (missing CommunityDatabase/DialectSelector registration, missing modern processSql overload, deprecated render() signature, duplicate function-factory calls) that align with or extend the review's spirit. But against an unusually rich human reference (30+ concrete points on type-mapping bugs, redundant super-handled registrations, code style, minimum version, untested '%' functions), v8 missed the bulk of the real defects. The dateadd/datediff and contributeTypes points are hedged speculation; supportsSubqueryOnMutatingTable is unverifiable from the truncation. Net negative due to heavy missed count on a deep review.
